query: here's the thing nagging at me.  'freshness' has a component of timeliness, no doubt.  However, it is also in utilization.  AKA. . We've build this knowledge base (Unimatrix) for long term memory for things that agents working in this project needs to know.  Under active development, (daily) a time based factor makes sense (and when we started, we did not have session data.  Fast forward... we now have session data.  I could just not work on this for 2 weeks, and every item would be stale (maybe thats not that big of a deal, because everything would age equally).  But... what if we thought about this problem more as a measure of activity utilization vs time based, or only time based

entries.title: ADR-002 col-031: Time-Based Retention Window for Frequency Table Rebuild
ADR-001 col-025: Goal Stored on cycle_events Start Row, Not sessions Table
Default freshness half-life (168h) miscalibrated for durable knowledge bases — silently kills ADR and convention confidence
Context load and cold restart hotspots scale with component count -- normalize before flagging
CO_ACCESS_STALENESS_SECONDS: 30-day window is too aggressive for dormant feature cycles
